problem_statement:
`fx.GraphModule` serialization fails when type annotations are `str`s

### 🐛 Describe the bug

```python
import torch                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              

def f(x: "torch.Tensor"):
    pass

gm = torch.fx.symbolic_trace(f)
torch.save(gm, "gm.pt")
```

### Error logs

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "bug.py", line 7, in <module>
    torch.save(gm, "gm.pt")
    ~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/.../lib/python3.13/site-packages/torch/serialization.py", line 977, in save
    _save(
    ~~~~~^
        obj,
        ^^^^
    ...<3 lines>...
        _disable_byteorder_record,
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    )
    ^
  File "/.../lib/python3.13/site-packages/torch/serialization.py", line 1227, in _save
    pickler.dump(obj)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^
  File "/.../lib/python3.13/site-packages/torch/fx/graph_module.py", line 980, in __reduce__
    import_block = _format_import_block(python_code.globals, sys_importer)
  File "/.../lib/python3.13/site-packages/torch/fx/graph_module.py", line 136, in _format_import_block
    _format_import_statement(name, obj, importer) for name, obj in globals.items()
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/.../lib/python3.13/site-packages/torch/fx/graph_module.py", line 130, in _format_import_statement
    module_name, attr_name = importer.get_name(obj)
                             ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^
  File "/.../lib/python3.13/site-packages/torch/package/importer.py", line 95, in get_name
    name = obj.__name__
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute '__name__'. Did you mean: '__ne__'?
```

patch:
diff --git a/torch/fx/graph_module.py b/torch/fx/graph_module.py
index 6cf6c2418fa4c..4c90b78da24ea 100644
--- a/torch/fx/graph_module.py
+++ b/torch/fx/graph_module.py
@@ -177,9 +177,13 @@ def _format_import_statement(name: str, obj: object, importer: Importer) -> str:
 
 
 def _format_import_block(globals: dict[str, Any], importer: Importer) -> str:
-    import_strs: set[str] = {
-        _format_import_statement(name, obj, importer) for name, obj in globals.items()
-    }
+    import_strs: set[str] = set()
+    for name, obj in globals.items():
+        if isinstance(obj, str):
+            # Forward-reference string annotations are globals in generated code.
+            import_strs.add(f"{name} = {obj!r}")
+        else:
+            import_strs.add(_format_import_statement(name, obj, importer))
     # Sort the imports so we have a stable import block that allows us to
     # hash the graph module and get a consistent key for use in a cache.
     return "\n".join(sorted(import_strs))
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/test_fx.py b/test/test_fx.py
index d455d10b5a5b2..68fcc10c58933 100644
--- a/test/test_fx.py
+++ b/test/test_fx.py
@@ -1908,6 +1908,42 @@ def test_pickle_nonetype_annotation(self):
         offsets = torch.LongTensor([0, 4])
         self.assertEqual(loaded(input, offsets), traced(input, offsets))
 
+    def test_save_string_type_annotation(self):
+        def f(x: "torch.Tensor") -> "torch.Tensor":
+            return x
+
+        traced = symbolic_trace(f)
+        _, (body, import_block) = traced.__reduce__()
+        self.assertExpectedInline(
+            import_block + body["_code"].rstrip(),
+            """\
+NoneType = type(None)
+_torch_Tensor_ = 'torch.Tensor'
+from math import inf
+from math import nan
+from torch import device
+import torch
+import torch.fx._pytree as fx_pytree
+import torch.utils._pytree as pytree
+
+
+def forward(self, x : _torch_Tensor_) -> _torch_Tensor_:
+    return x""",
+        )
+
+        bio = io.BytesIO()
+        torch.save(traced, bio)
+        bio.seek(0)
+        loaded = torch.load(bio, weights_only=False)
+        loaded.graph.lint()
+
+        x = torch.randn(2, 3)
+        self.assertEqual(loaded(x), traced(x))
+        self.assertEqual(
+            loaded.forward.__annotations__,
+            {"x": "torch.Tensor", "return": "torch.Tensor"},
+        )
+
     def test_return_tuple(self):
         class M(torch.nn.Module):
             def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
